results.title: Hiring? Looking for work? [Startup Roles June 2026] - Product Hunt
results.content:
I build things. Over the past few months I've shipped a weather prediction market trading agent on Kalshi, an autonomous equity trading agent on Alpaca (up ~25% in 2 months), a K1 document OCR pipeline, and most recently Dreams of Yesterday — an AI pipeline that turns family stories into cinematic films.

Not looking to leave tomorrow but open to the right conversations. If you're building in AI, legal tech, financial services AI, or consumer memory/legacy tech and need someone who can build the product AND walk into a room with institutional clients and be credible -that's the zone I work in.

Launching Dreams of Yesterday today on my profile page!.

Find me: · github.com/jzrucker - www.linkedin.com/in/joseph-zeke-rucker/

Dmitrii

Hi everyone! I'm Dmitrii. [...] ### Design & Creative

### Top forum threads [...] Capacitor (for Android apps) & Google Chrome Extensions

My Featured Project: Recently I develop Mouzi - a free, privacy-first, local-first automatic file organizer that quietly sits in the system tray and keeps your Downloads folder sorted. It became quite a hit, and building it from the ground up gave me solid experience in delivering tools that users actually love and find genuinely useful.

What I'm Looking For:

Full-time roles in a fast-paced environment.

Part-time / freelance gigs (website admin, web dev, vibecoding) that I can do after my regular working hours.

Let's Connect!

Portfolio: 

GitHub: github.com/hsr88

Joe Rucker

Hey - Zeke here. Active CPA/JD working at the intersection of institutional finance and AI at a national consulting firm.

results.title: Kilo Code Reviewer - 24 hours on Product Hunt : Kilo Code Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
### AI Agents

### Trending categories

### Top reviewed

### Trending products

### Top forum threads [...] No pressure.

In January 2026, they launched Kilo Code Reviewer - AI-powered code review agents that understand your codebase and catch bugs before merging.

I experimented with something new, live blogging the launch for the next 24 hours.

Let's go.

## TL,DR

12:01:AM:PST - going live!

04:12:AM:PST - new milestone: 200 points

06:17:AM:PST - new milestone: 300 points

09:39:AM:PST - new milestone: 400 points

02:34:PM:PST - new milestone: 500 points

11:44:PM:PST - new milestone: 600 points

## 12:01:AM:PST - going live

Step 1: getting featured. Check!

For the first 4 hours of the day, Product Hunt hides upvotes and sorts the homepage randomly. This should give products "a more distributed chance at exposure early." - Source [...] That's a wrap!

Results

636 points

115 comments

#1 Product of the Day - featured in The Leaderboard, Product Hunt's daily newsletter

1 ,002 new followers on Product Hunt (+43.3%)

Key takeaway

Launching multiple times create a tailwind.

When first launched a year ago, @Kilo Code had 0 followers, ranked #5 Product of the Day. Later on, they launched their @JetBrains extension and ranked #1 Product of the Day.

Launch, and keep launching. Product Hunt pays off in the long term.

Oh and they're still #3 Product of the Month. Join the launch →
